blocks.text:
To: Jason Myers
Cc: Jeffrey Patterson, James Grant

Thank you for the session on Tuesday. It was more useful than these usually are, largely because your people disagreed with each other in front of us rather than afterwards. I would far rather see that now than at the finalist stage.

Since the briefing we have kept the approach calls running against the signed specification. James Grant has had first conversations across the segment we described to you as sitting one tier up, and Jeffrey Patterson has closed the remaining gaps in the map on the divisional side. I am not going to characterize the shape of the slate yet. We are early, and an early read has a way of becoming the client's expectation before it has earned it. What I will say is that the universe is behaving the way the map said it would, which is the first good sign in any search.

Two asks. First, we need your position on relocation in principle, not in detail, before the longlist review. A meaningful part of the qualified universe for {{fact:f:E-2015-001.client}} sits outside your metropolitan area, and I do not want to spend approach calls on people you cannot actually move. Second, please have your office hold provisional finalist dates now. Every search I have lost to a calendar, I lost in the week nobody thought the calendar mattered.

One housekeeping note. Anything we send about candidates goes to you and the panel only, and describes people by their seat. If someone at Roach, Moss and Hall outside that group asks for a copy, send them to me rather than forwarding it. It is not bureaucracy; it is the reason good people take our calls.
